title: Creative Engineering
description: A starter pack for the wonderful Create mod, including overhauled terrain generation! There's enough variety to keep you entertained, while also keeping it very simple.
body:
![Oceanside Forest](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/ggiEtH2g/images/4ea7abe47cab8b74bb421f4f4a03585cfcd721de.png)
# Creative Engineering

A **1.20.1 Fabric Modpack** designed to let your creativity flow without hardware limitations! Created with low-end hardware in mind, Creative Engineering lets you play **Create** however you want. There are still other content mods, such as **Terralith** and **Farmer's Delight** to promote exploring and to just have a good time. With playability and explorability in mind, there is so much spread throughout the world to go and find. With mods such as **Camera Utils** and **Iris**, the Creative Engineering experience will be unique to say the least.

## Features

<details>
<summary>World/Gameplay</summary>

- Create
- Create Slice & Dice
- Create Mod Trims
- Create Jetpack
- Farmer's Delight
- Chef's Delight
- Terralith
- Incendium
- Nullscape
- Tectonic
- Chipped
- Decorative Blocks
- Nullscape
- Chunkloaders

</details>
<details>
<summary>Utility/Optimization</summary>

- Better F3
- Continuity
- More Culling
- Simple Voice Chat
- Sodium
- Sodium Extra
- Exordium
- AppleSkin
- Starlight
- Entity Culling
- Enhanced Block Entities
- Iris
- Mouse Tweaks
- Jade
- Jade Addons
- Lithium
- Cull Less Leaves
- JourneyMap
- EmoteCraft
- Camera Utils
- Just Enough Items

</details>

## Summary

Obviously, this is a **Create** mod focused modpack, but it isn't just that. There are so many opportunities to explore the world, and just build using **Chipped** and **Decorative Blocks!**

## Performance

Built on Fabric, this modpack was intended to perform really well, and I hope that it does for you. It is a bit of a RAM hog, so if you're running a server I'd recommend 4 GB, but I run mine with 3 GB just fine (kinda).

In some very lame and half-baked testing, it is pretty safe to say that when using the modpack to just play on low settings, I found I got consistently above 40 FPS on an **Intel Core-i3 10105** processor and an **Intel UHD 630** integrated graphics sytem.
